description_long: This is a small, neoclassical bank building with a prominent triangular pediment supported by two rectangular columns at the front. The facade features a central doorway framed by the columns and a circular decorative element set into the pediment. The structure is constructed from a uniform, gray stone or concrete material, giving it a solid and imposing appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and symmetrical, with clean, sharp lines and no visible windows or ornamentation on the sides or rear. The base of the building is slightly raised, with a simple plinth running around the perimeter. The overall design is minimalistic, evoking the traditional architecture of historic banks.
description: A compact, neoclassical bank building with a triangular pediment, two front columns, and a central doorway, all rendered in gray stone. The structure is solid, symmetrical, and minimally ornamented.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triangular pediment, two supporting columns, a central doorway, and a circular decorative element above the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, flat wall with no windows or decorative features, and the edge of the roof overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a featureless back wall and the continuation of the pitched roof, with no visible entrance or ornamentation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing a plain wall and the roof's overhang.
attribute_entrance_design: Single central door, rectangular, likely solid material, framed by two columns and topped with a circular decorative element in the pediment.
